post.description:
Clear this device's cookies AND server-side revoke every outstanding
token for the user by bumping ``token_version``. Best-effort on the bump:
a missing/expired/invalid token still clears cookies (idempotent logout),
it just can't invalidate other devices.

schema.title: Response List Stores Api V1 Stores Get
Response List Cameras Api V1 Cameras Get

get.description:
Mint a short-lived WHEP/HLS read token for a camera the caller owns.

Any org member (read access) may watch; the token confines playback to this
camera's mediamtx_path and is validated by the MediaMTX authHTTP endpoint.

post.description: Multipart upload of an mp4. Triggers sentry-ai via separate /verify call.

schema.title: Response List Clips Api V1 Clips Get
Response List Alerts Api V1 Alerts Get

post.description:
MediaMTX authHTTP authorizer (replaces MediaMTX's open `user: any` read).

- read/playback → require a valid per-camera stream token in the query
- publish       → MediaMTX publish creds
- api/metrics/pprof → MediaMTX control-API creds
Returns 204 to allow, 401 to deny. Optionally gated by a shared secret so
only the MediaMTX host can call it.

sentry-ai POSTs here after VLM verification finishes.

We derive store/camera/org from the clip — sentry-ai doesn't need to
know the tenancy hierarchy.

Node-push live alert. The AI node detected the breach, cut + VLM-verified
the clip locally, and POSTs the finished result here (outbound — reliable,
unlike the old cloud→node /v1/cut-verify pull). camera_id = mediamtx_path.

sentry-ai live worker POSTs batched per-frame metadata here.

Each frame is (a) dispatched to subscribers of its `camera_id` (browser
WebSocket clients on `/ws/live/{camera_id}`) and (b) fed through the
threshold handler to fire alerts on sustained risk_pct breaches (L5).

schema.title: Response Receive Live Metadata Api V1 Internal Live Metadata Post

post.description:
Store a staff-verified event (embedded by the AI node) for future RAG
retrieval. Called when feedback confirms/denies a suspicion.

schema.title: Response Rag Add Case Api V1 Internal Rag Cases Post

post.description:
Top-k past verified cases most similar to the query embedding — the AI
node injects these into the VLM verify prompt as few-shot examples.

schema.title: Response Rag Similar Api V1 Internal Rag Similar Post

get.description:
Alert breakdown for the observability dashboard (docs/19 Phase 2): how
many suspicion alerts fired in `range`, split by VLM category, by review
level, and by day — so you see WHAT the system is catching, over time.

schema.title: Response Alert Analytics Api V1 Admin Analytics Alerts Get

get.description:
Close the feedback loop (docs/19 Phase 3). Joins staff verdicts (Feedback)
to the alert's VLM category and reports, per category, how many were marked
true_positive / false_positive / unclear + the false-alarm rate — plus
read-only TUNING SUGGESTIONS for noisy categories (high FP rate). Applying a
suggestion (weight/threshold change) stays a human action for now.

schema.title: Response Feedback Analytics Api V1 Admin Analytics Feedback Get
Response List Orgs Api V1 Admin Orgs Get

delete.description:
Delete a tenant entirely (T15 #3 — the privacy policy's deletion promise).

Every org-scoped row cascades in the DB; the org's clip evidence FILES are
unlinked from disk first (a cascade can't reach the filesystem).

schema.title: Response List Org Members Api V1 Admin Orgs  Org Id  Members Get
Response List Users Api V1 Admin Users Get
Response List Leads Api V1 Admin Leads Get
Response List Ai Nodes Api V1 Admin Ai Nodes Get

get.description:
Resource time-series (CPU/RAM/GPU) for the observability dashboard.

`range` = 1h | 6h | 24h | 7d | 30d. `bucket` = auto | raw | hour (auto picks
raw for ≤24h, hourly averages for wider ranges to keep the payload small).

schema.title: Response Ai Node Metrics Api V1 Admin Ai Nodes  Node Id  Metrics Get

get.description: Wallet balance + status + the per-store rate breakdown.
Ledger entries for the org, newest first.

schema.title: Response List Journal Api V1 Billing Journal Get
Response Org Journal Api V1 Admin Billing Orgs  Org Id  Journal Get

post.description: Record a received payment: Dr cash / Cr org_wallet, balance += amount.
Emergency unlock: keep a non-paying org alive until `until`.

schema.title: Response List Promo Codes Api V1 Admin Billing Promo Codes Get

get.description: Daily money flow (usage / topup / promo) for the superadmin chart.
Members of the caller's org (any member may view their own team).

schema.title: Response List Members Api V1 Org Members Get

delete.description:
Owner deletes their own organization (T15 #3 — privacy policy promise).

Owner-only (admins manage the team; only the owner can erase the tenant).
The caller must retype the org slug (`confirm_slug`) so a stray DELETE can't
wipe everything. All org data cascades in the DB; clip evidence files are
removed from disk first. Members keep their user accounts but lose all
org-scoped access the moment the membership rows cascade away.

schema.title: Response List Invitations Api V1 Org Invitations Get

post.description:
Invite an email to the caller's org. Emails a tokenized accept link;
if SMTP isn't configured the link is returned for manual sharing.

delete.description: Revoke a pending invite. The token immediately stops working.

patch.description:
Lock (is_active=false) or unlock a member. Locking blocks their login
immediately without deleting the account. Can't lock yourself or the owner.

post.description: Public — the invitee sets their password to create the account + join.

schema.title: Response List Store Agents Api V1 Stores  Store Id  Agents Get
Response Agent List Cameras Api V1 Agent Cameras Get

patch.description:
Edit a camera's connection (rtsp_url), display name, or risk threshold.

Used by the desktop agent's 'Засах' (edit) flow when a camera's IP or
credentials change — so the user can fix the connection without delete +
re-add (which would churn the UUID + mediamtx_path). When the rtsp_url
changes we re-provision the live worker so the AI pulls the new source.

get.description:
Tell a paired agent where (and whether) to publish its camera streams.

push_enabled=True → cloud topology: the agent runs ffmpeg relays pushing
each LAN camera to `push_rtsp_base/<mediamtx_path>`. False → MediaMTX pulls
cameras directly (local/on-LAN) and the agent pushes nothing.

post.description: Report telemetry + receive the latest config in one round-trip.

patch.description: Bulk weights (existing keys) + thresholds + engine knobs. Super-admin only.

schema.title: Response List Org Events Api V1 Events Get
Response List Admin Events Api V1 Admin Events Get

AcceptInvite.title: AcceptInvite
AcceptInvite.description: Public — the invitee sets their own password to activate the account.

AdminStats.title: AdminStats
AdminStats.description: Top-level counts for the super-admin dashboard.

AgentCameraCreate.title: AgentCameraCreate
AgentCameraCreate.description:
Camera registration payload from a paired agent (store comes from the
agent's token, not the body).

AgentCameraUpdate.title: AgentCameraUpdate
AgentCameraUpdate.description:
Partial camera update from a paired agent (edit connection / settings).

All fields optional — only the provided ones change. mediamtx_path is NOT
editable here: it is the stream identity the live pipeline keys on, so the
agent edits the connection (rtsp_url) and name, never the path.

AgentPairResult.title: AgentPairResult
AgentPairResult.description: Returned to the agent on successful pairing.

AgentPublic.title: AgentPublic
AgentPublic.description: A paired agent, shown in the admin UI.

AgentStreamConfig.title: AgentStreamConfig

AiNodePairRequest.title: AiNodePairRequest
AiNodePairRequest.description: Posted by the installed sentry-ai server with the 6-digit code.

cameras.title: Cameras
cameras.description:
Per-camera stream health parsed from the latest heartbeat telemetry
(audit T12 #3). None for old node versions / no telemetry yet — the
superadmin UI can distinguish "unknown" from "no cameras" ([]).

provider_effective.title: Provider Effective
provider_effective.description:
VLM provider the node actually runs (from its last heartbeat). The UI
compares this to `provider` (desired) to show applied vs applying.

provider_error.title: Provider Error
provider_error.description: Why the effective provider isn't ready (e.g. model not pulled), or None.

breach_mode_effective.title: Breach Mode Effective
breach_mode_effective.description:
Live-breach topology the node actually applied (from its last
heartbeat). The UI compares this to `breach_mode` (desired) to show
applied vs applying. None for old node versions / no telemetry yet.

is_online.title: Is Online
is_online.description: Server-computed online status — independent of the viewer's clock.

BehaviorConfigPatch.title: BehaviorConfigPatch
BehaviorConfigPatch.description: Bulk update — weights (existing keys only), thresholds, and/or engine knobs.

BehaviorDetailItem.title: BehaviorDetailItem
BehaviorDetailItem.description: One row of the alert's behaviour timeline (first-fired order).

CameraHealth.title: CameraHealth
CameraHealth.description:
Per-camera stream health inside the node heartbeat (audit T12 #3).

Lets the cloud tell WHICH camera died instead of only seeing the summed
FPS dip. status: ok = inferring, stalled = worker alive but 0 FPS,
error = worker dead or RTSP read/open failure.

CameraPublic.title: CameraPublic
CameraPublic.description: Public Camera — `rtsp_url` NEVER exposed; only a boolean `has_rtsp_url`.

ComponentUsage.title: ComponentUsage
ComponentUsage.description:
Per-component CPU/RAM (sentry-ai / Ollama / MediaMTX / Tunnel) — the
resource breakdown shown in the dashboard. Stored in telemetry JSON.

InviteCreate.title: InviteCreate
InviteCreate.description: Org owner/admin invites an email to their org with a role.

InviteResult.title: InviteResult
InviteResult.description:
Returned after creating an invite. `invite_url` lets the admin share the
link manually when email isn't configured (`emailed=false`).

LeadCreate.title: LeadCreate
LeadCreate.description:
Public landing-page submission.

`website` is a honeypot — a hidden field real users never fill. Bots that
blindly fill every input trip it; the endpoint silently drops those.

LeadUpdate.title: LeadUpdate
LeadUpdate.description: Super-admin triage — change status and/or attach notes.

LiveAlertCreate.title: LiveAlertCreate
LiveAlertCreate.description:
POST /api/v1/internal/live-alert — node-push path (ADR live-alert).

The AI node detected a sustained risk breach, cut the clip + ran VLM
LOCALLY, and posts the finished alert here (outbound, reliable). camera_id
is the Camera.mediamtx_path; the backend resolves org/store. The node only
posts non-ignore verdicts (it applies the VLM gate itself).

LiveFrame.title: LiveFrame
LiveFrame.description:
Per-analyzed-frame metadata from the sentry-ai live worker
(REV.1 — mirrors sentry-ai's FrameMetadata).

LiveMetadataBatch.title: LiveMetadataBatch
LiveMetadataBatch.description: Batch of per-frame metadata from sentry-ai live worker.

LiveTrack.title: LiveTrack
LiveTrack.description:
One detected person in one frame (REV.1 — mirrors sentry-ai's
TrackPayload). Lenient: unknown keys ignored, display fields defaulted so a
schema addition on the AI side never 422s the whole batch.

MediaMtxAuthRequest.title: MediaMtxAuthRequest
MediaMtxAuthRequest.description:
MediaMTX authHTTP payload (subset we use). MediaMTX POSTs this on every
publish/read/playback/api action when `authMethod: http`.

MemberUpdate.title: MemberUpdate
MemberUpdate.description: Org admin toggles a member's access (lock = is_active False).

OrgDeleteConfirm.title: OrgDeleteConfirm
OrgDeleteConfirm.description:
Owner deletes their own org — must retype the org slug to confirm.

Guards against an accidental DELETE (fat-finger, replayed request) wiping a
whole tenant: the slug is something only a deliberate caller types in.

OrgMemberPublic.title: OrgMemberPublic
OrgMemberPublic.description: A user together with their role inside one organization.

PairingCodePublic.title: PairingCodePublic
PairingCodePublic.description: Returned to the admin UI after generating a code.

RagCaseCreate.title: RagCaseCreate
RagCaseCreate.description: Store a verified event the AI node has embedded.

RagSimilarRequest.title: RagSimilarRequest
RagSimilarRequest.description: Ask for the most similar past verified cases.

StoreBillingLine.title: StoreBillingLine
StoreBillingLine.description: Per-store breakdown shown on the customer billing page.

StreamTokenResponse.title: StreamTokenResponse
StreamTokenResponse.description: Short-lived per-camera WHEP/HLS read token (appended as ?jwt=…).

UserAdminUpdate.title: UserAdminUpdate
UserAdminUpdate.description:
Partial update of a user's super-admin / active flags.

Both fields optional; only provided keys are applied.

VlmStatus.title: VlmStatus
VlmStatus.description:
Whether a VLM model is resident in Ollama + its GPU split, so the dashboard
can show the VLM's real GPU/VRAM footprint (per-process VRAM isn't on WDDM).
